summary:
### 综合概述：智能代理方法论、智能代理及逻辑推理智能代理

在这三份文档中，我们讨论了智能代理的方法论及其在环境中的应用。《L13 - Methodologies.pdf》主要关注的是智能代理的方法论。它指出当前的面向对象（OO）方法学对于实现智能代理存在不足，因为传统的类、对象和客户端/服务架构并不能直接适应智能代理的需求。文档进一步阐述了不同的智能代理类型及其特点，并讨论了移动代理的相关问题与类型。

《L2.0 - Intelligent Agents (1).pdf》则强调了智能代理的本质以及它们在环境中的自主性行为，具体而言，它是通过一系列决策实现其目标的计算机系统。文档还介绍了简单的代理类型如恒温器（物理环境）。此外，《L3 - Deductive Reasoning Agents (1).pdf》进一步探讨了代理方法论、符号推理以及逻辑推理性智能代理的应用。

这三个文件之间存在一些重叠的观点，例如都强调智能代理在自主决策和实现任务上的能力。但也有明显的差异：文档一特别关注智能代理的方法论及不同类型的智能代理，而文档三则深入介绍了基于逻辑的推理技术及其如何用于开发智能代理。《L2.0 - Intelligent Agents (1).pdf》中的重点是识别代理行为与对象、人工智能系统之间的对比，并说明为何智能代理可以被视为一种有意图的系统。

### 参考文献

- L13 - Methodologies.pdf
- L2.0 - Intelligent Agents (1).pdf
- L3 - Deductive Reasoning Agents (1).pdf

comparison.name: L13 - Methodologies.pdf
comparison.mainPoints: METHODOLOGIES BAXI 3113 – INTELLIGENT AGENT Dr. Nur Zareen Zulkarnain Department of Intelligent Computing and Analytics (ICA) DG/1 -18 zareen@utem.edu.my 1 LEARNING OUTCOMES At the end of this lecture, you should be able to: •Understand the need of agent -oriented methodologies •State different types of agent -oriented methodologies •Explain mobile agents by discussing the needs, issues and typology associated to it •Differentiate Telescript and TCL/TK 2 AGENT ORIENTED METHODOLOGIES •There is need for agent -oriented methodologies → centred around specific agent -oriented abstractions •The adoption of OO methodologies would produce mismatches - Classes, objects, client -servers: little to do with agents 3 AGENT ORIENTED METHODOLOGIES •Each methodology may introduce further abstractions - around which to model software and to organize the software process •For example: roles, organizations, responsibilities, belief, desire and intentions •Not directly translating into concrete entities of the software system → the concept of role is an aspect of an agent , not an agent 4 AGENT -BASED ANALYSIS •Analysis aims to understand , at least •What are the main actors interacting with the system •How the system interacts with these actors •What the system is supposed to do •Then, we associate accordingly •Roles responsibilities and capabilities •Interaction patterns between agents 5 AGENT -BASED DESIGN •Design aims to engineer , at least •What are the main components interacting within the system •What are the responsibilities and the capabilities of each component in the system •How the components interact to implement the system, i.e., the architecture of the system •Differently from analysis: we need to choose on which agents to use and how they interact 6 RELEVANT METHODOLOGIES •GAIA Methodologies •Encourages a developer to think of building agent -based systems as a process of organisational design •Agent UML •Extend Unified Modeling Language (UML) with agent features •Prometeus •Focuses mainly on the internal agent architecture → basically a methodology for designing BDI agent systems 7 MOBILE AGENTS 8 9REMOTE PROCEDURE CALLSclient computer server computer network client process server process MOBILE AGENTSclient computer server computer networkclient process serverprocessagent vs MOBILE AGENTS Why mobile agents? •low-bandwidth networks •efficient use of network resources There are many issues when building software tools that can support mobile agents: •security for hosts and agents •heterogeneity of hosts •dynamic linking 10 MOBILE AGENT TYPOLOGY 11 We can divide mobile agents into at least three types Autonomous On-demand ‘Active mail’ AUTONOMOUS •By autonomous mobile, we mean agents that are able to decide for themselves where to go, when, and what to do when they get there (subject to certain resource constraints) •Such agents are generally programmed in a special language that provides a go instruction •best known example is TELESCRIPT 12 ON-DEMAND •The idea is that a host is only required to execute an agent when it explicitly demands the agent •Best known example → JAVA in html •A user with a JAVA -compatible browser can request html pages that contain applets – small programs implemented in the JAVA language •These applets are downloaded along with all other images, text, forms, etc., on the page, and, once downloaded, are executed on the user’s machine 13 ‘ACTIVE MAIL’ •The idea here is to ‘piggy -back’ agent programs onto mail •The best -known example of this work is the mime extension to email, allowing Safe -TCL scripts to be sent •When email is received, the ‘agent’ is unpacked, and the script executed → hence the email is no longer passive, but active 14 TELESCRIPT AND TCL/TK 15 TELESCRIPT •TELESCRIPT is a language -based environment for constructing mobile agent systems •There are two key concepts in TELESCRIPT technology: •Places → virtual locations occupied by agents.

comparison.name: L2.0 - Intelligent Agents (1).pdf
comparison.mainPoints: INTELLIGENT AGENTS BAXI 3113 – INTELLIGENT AGENT Dr. Nur Zareen Zulkarnain Department of Intelligent Computing and Analytics (ICA) DG/1 -18 zareen@utem.edu.my 1 LEARNING OUTCOMES At the end of today’s lecture, you should be able to: •Recognise agent behaviours •Compare agent with objects and AI systems •Justify agents as intentional system •Identify tasks for agents 2 WHAT IS AN AGENT? 3 WHAT IS AN AGENT?
comparison.conclusions: INTELLIGENT AGENTS BAXI 3113 – INTELLIGENT AGENT Dr. Nur Zareen Zulkarnain Department of Intelligent Computing and Analytics (ICA) DG/1 -18 zareen@utem.edu.my 1 LEARNING OUTCOMES At the end of today’s lecture, you should be able to: •Recognise agent behaviours •Compare agent with objects and AI systems •Justify agents as intentional system •Identify tasks for agents 2 WHAT IS AN AGENT? 3 WHAT IS AN AGENT?

comparison.name: L3 - Deductive Reasoning Agents (1).pdf
comparison.mainPoints: DEDUCTIVE REASONING AGENTS BAXI 3113 – INTELLIGENT AGENT Dr. Nur Zareen Zulkarnain Department of Intelligent Computing and Analytics (ICA) DG/1 -18 zareen@utem.edu.my 1 RECAP •Agent behaviors – Reactive, Pro -active, Social •Properties of environment – Real time, Dynamic, Episodic etc. •Compare agent with objects and AI systems •Agents as intentional system •Tasks for agents – Achievement Task vs Management Task •Abstract architectures for agents 2 LEARNING OUTCOMES At the end of today’s lecture, you should be able to: •Describe agent architectures •Explain the notion of symbolic reasoning agents •Show how deductive reasoning can be achieved using logic •Differentiate types of Agent Oriented Programming 3 AGENT ARCHITECTURES 4 AGENT ARCHITECTURES “… [A] particular methodology for building [agents].

summary:
智能体（Intelligent Agent）是当今智能计算领域的核心概念，这三份文档从不同维度对其进行了深入探讨，涵盖了其基本定义、开发方法论以及内部推理机制。

首先，根据文档2《智能体》，智能体被定义为一个位于特定环境中，能够自主行动以实现其既定目标的计算机系统。其核心特征在于“自主

comparison.conclusions: METHODOLOGIES BAXI 3113 – INTELLIGENT AGENT Dr. Nur Zareen Zulkarnain Department of Intelligent Computing and Analytics (ICA) DG/1 -18 zareen@utem.edu.my 1 LEARNING OUTCOMES At the end of this lecture, you should be able to: •Understand the need of agent -oriented methodologies •State different types of agent -oriented methodologies •Explain mobile agents by discussing the needs, issues and typology associated to it •Differentiate Telescript and TCL/TK 2 AGENT ORIENTED METHODOLOGIES •There is need for agent -oriented methodologies → centred around specific agent -oriented abstractions •The adoption of OO methodologies would produce mismatches - Classes, objects, client -servers: little to do with agents 3 AGENT ORIENTED METHODOLOGIES •Each methodology may introduce further abstractions - around which to model software and to organize the software process •For example: roles, organizations, responsibilities, belief, desire and intentions •Not directly translating into concrete entities of the software system → the concept of role is an aspect of an agent , not an agent 4 AGENT -BASED ANALYSIS •Analysis aims to understand , at least •What are the main actors interacting with the system •How the system interacts with these actors •What the system is supposed to do •Then, we associate accordingly •Roles responsibilities and capabilities •Interaction patterns between agents 5 AGENT -BASED DESIGN •Design aims to engineer , at least •What are the main components interacting within the system •What are the responsibilities and the capabilities of each component in the system •How the components interact to implement the system, i.e., the architecture of the system •Differently from analysis: we need to choose on which agents to use and how they interact 6 RELEVANT METHODOLOGIES •GAIA Methodologies •Encourages a developer to think of building agent -based systems as a process of organisational design •Agent UML •Extend Unified Modeling Language (UML) with agent features •Prometeus •Focuses mainly on the internal agent architecture → basically a methodology for designing BDI agent systems 7 MOBILE AGENTS 8 9REMOTE PROCEDURE CALLSclient computer server computer network client process server process MOBILE AGENTSclient computer server computer networkclient process serverprocessagent vs MOBILE AGENTS Why mobile agents? •low-bandwidth networks •efficient use of network resources There are many issues when building software tools that can support mobile agents: •security for hosts and agents •heterogeneity of hosts •dynamic linking 10 MOBILE AGENT TYPOLOGY 11 We can divide mobile agents into at least three types Autonomous On-demand ‘Active mail’ AUTONOMOUS •By autonomous mobile, we mean agents that are able to decide for themselves where to go, when, and what to do when they get there (subject to certain resource constraints) •Such agents are generally programmed in a special language that provides a go instruction •best known example is TELESCRIPT 12 ON-DEMAND •The idea is that a host is only required to execute an agent when it explicitly demands the agent •Best known example → JAVA in html •A user with a JAVA -compatible browser can request html pages that contain applets – small programs implemented in the JAVA language •These applets are downloaded along with all other images, text, forms, etc., on the page, and, once downloaded, are executed on the user’s machine 13 ‘ACTIVE MAIL’ •The idea here is to ‘piggy -back’ agent programs onto mail •The best -known example of this work is the mime extension to email, allowing Safe -TCL scripts to be sent •When email is received, the ‘agent’ is unpacked, and the script executed → hence the email is no longer passive, but active 14 TELESCRIPT AND TCL/TK 15 TELESCRIPT •TELESCRIPT is a language -based environment for constructing mobile agent systems •There are two key concepts in TELESCRIPT technology: •Places → virtual locations occupied by agents.

[L1- Introduction to Agents (1).pdf] INTRODUCTION TO AGENTS BAXI 3113 – INTELLIGENT AGENT Dr. Nur Zareen Zulkarnain Department of Intelligent Computing and Analytics (ICA) DG/1 -18 zareen@utem.edu.my 1 Students will be exposed to the concept of intelligent agent and multiagent systems including: theory of agents common agent architectures methods of cooperationmethods of communication potential applications for agentsCOURSE OVERVIEW 2 COURSE LEARNING OUTCOMES Upon completing this subject, you should be able to: CLO1: Evaluate various concepts of intelligent agent. CLO2: Organise effective solution steps in solving intelligent agent problems.
